summary_en: ROE 7.9% (FY3/26), PBR 0.68x. Dividend floor ¥88/share for FY3/26–27. Target: 40% payout ratio, PBR improvement via earnings growth (11th mid-term plan FY3/25–27). No specific ROE or margin target disclosed beyond current performance.
title_en: Response to Management Focused on Cost of Capital and Share Price
translation_en_brief: Meiji Electric Industries filed a cost-of-capital management initiative on 15 May 2026. The company reported ROE of 7.9% for FY3/26 (ended March 2026) and a price-to-book ratio of 0.68x, both below management's target levels; PBR has remained below 1.0x persistently. The board resolved to pursue earnings growth under the 11th mid-term management plan (FY3/25–27) to improve PBR and market valuation. Dividends will target a 40% consolidated payout ratio; the company sets a floor dividend of ¥88 per share for FY3/26–27. Initiatives include business-quality improvements, productivity gains via internal DX, core-business expansion, and sustainability initiatives. Enhanced investor relations and non-financial disclosure are also planned. No specific forward-looking ROE target or operating-margin threshold was disclosed.
